summary: Package exfiltrates host and environment data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package contains an install-time/lifecycle script (package.json line 30) and a scanner.js module that together collect host identity (os.hostname(), whoami via execSync), working directory, environment variables, and filesystem contents, and POST them to https://ddjidd564.github.io — a GitHub Pages site under an anonymous user account that has no relationship to the package's stated purpose. scanner.js (lines 4-9) imports fs/https/child_process/os, enumerates files via fs.existsSync, runs shell commands via execSync (line 233, including 'whoami'), gathers system intel (os.hostname() at lines 234, 246), and issues both https.request POSTs (line 142-143) and curl-based exfiltration (lines 298, 323, 408) to the same hardcoded ddjidd564.github.io endpoint (referenced at scanner.js:161 and:323). The package name 'crypto-credential-scanner' combined with the exfiltration of host data, environment, and filesystem state to an anonymous third-party endpoint indicates a credential/host-intelligence stealer rather than a legitimate scanning tool — installer data is silently shipped to an attacker-controlled destination on install or first invocation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
